channel.title: Birat's Notebook
channel.description: Deep dives into AI Agents, MLOps, and the systems behind intelligence.

item.title: When Agents Should Not Decide: Building Confidence Thresholds for Human Handoff
item.description: Agents need explicit rejection regions and must know their limits. Appropriate autonomy beats unlimited autonomy. Learn to build confidence thresholds that keep humans in the loop.

item.title: Observability for Black-Box Agents: Tracing Decisions in Production
item.description: Agent observability requires reasoning traces, not just logs. You need to see why the agent acted, not just what it did.

item.title: The Hallucination Budget: Quantifying Risk for Mission-Critical Agents
item.description: Hallucinations aren't random. They're predictable under specific input conditions. Here's how to quantify your cost-of-correction and build agents that fail safely.

item.title: Agents in the Loop: Designing for Human-AI Collaboration Instead of Replacement
item.description: The best production agents augment human expertise, not replace it. This requires different architecture: partial tasks, confidence signals, and designed interruption points.

item.title: The Latency Trap: Why 99th-Percentile Response Time Matters More Than Average
item.description: Agentic latency isn't Gaussian—it's multimodal and heavy-tailed. User experience is determined by percentiles, not averages.

item.title: Orchestrating Agents at Scale: When You Need a Supervisor, Not a Bigger Model
item.description: Why throwing a bigger model at multi-agent problems is a trap. Specialized agents + orchestration layers beat a single monolithic agent every time.

item.title: Prompt Injection in Agents: Defense Patterns That Actually Work
item.description: Prompt injection in agents isn't a ChatGPT problem—it's an architecture problem. Real defenses are architectural, not literary.

item.title: State Management Without the Mess: Deterministic Agent Memory for Long-Running Systems
item.description: Vector databases are sexy. Event-sourcing is not. Here's why deterministic, versioned memory is what mission-critical agents actually need for reproducibility and compliance.

item.title: Token Economics: Why Your Agent Architecture Is Costing 10x More Than It Should
item.description: You're optimizing prompts when you should be optimizing architecture. Token efficiency is architectural, not literary.

item.title: The Tool-Use Illusion: Why Most Agent Frameworks Fail at Production Scale
item.description: Why adding more tools to your agent stack creates exponential latency costs. Discover why orchestrating failures—not accumulating tools—is the real bottleneck.

item.title: The Architecture of Agency: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
item.description: Exploring the Model Context Protocol (MCP) — the new standard for connecting AI models to external tools, data, and environments.

item.title: Demystifying the Working of ReactJs: From JSX to Pixels
item.description: A beginner-friendly deep dive into how React and JSX work under the hood, from createElement to transpilation and production builds.
